title: Ageratina dombeyana
taxonomy: Ageratina dombeyana is classified in the family Asteraceae, which is one of the largest and most widespread families of flowering plants. The genus Ageratina is a part of this family and is native to the Americas. Ageratina dombeyana specifically is native to South America. This plant is further classified into the order Asterales, which is a group of flowering plants that are closely related to sunflowers and daisies. Ageratina dombeyana is a flowering plant species that belongs to the kingdom Plantae.
uses: Ageratina dombeyana, also known as Cachipora or Hierba de la reina, has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits. Its leaves and roots are rich in bioactive compounds, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In folk medicine, Ageratina dombeyana is used to treat digestive issues, such as diarrhea and gastritis, as well as to reduce inflammation and alleviate respiratory problems.
